cowardice_flags.avoided: No party in power has moved to pass it.
cowardice_flags.should_say: The current PH-led unity government under Anwar — elected in part on anti-1MDB reform promises, now coalitioned with BN — has stalled the political financing bill with no legislative timeline or draft tabled as of April 2026.

false_balance.where: Hook: 'One side has 76 witnesses and forged Saudi letters. The other has rally T-shirts.'
false_balance.evidence_says: Court found Saudi defence 'incapable of belief' with 'cold, hard and incontrovertible' evidence; rallies offer no legal equivalence to 25-count conviction.

audience_check: Ipoh taxi driver / Johor factory worker: nods at RM24B debt eating education spending and B40 pain; rolls eyes at the polite 'all parties' synthesis that dodges naming current unity government delays. Penang hawker / Sabah farmer: shares the T-shirts-vs-evidence hook but tunes out elite reframing as too abstract.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Decades of Bumiputera/NEP affirmative action disproportionately benefited connected Malay elites through patronage; B40 Bumiputera remain overrepresented in poverty and rural dependency.
Current PH-BN unity government maintains GLC and patronage appointments to hold coalition together, prioritizing stability over dismantling the system that enabled 1MDB.
Chinese business elites participate in cross-ethnic patronage; broader community faces systemic quotas/glass ceilings in public sector/education.

gender_truths_avoided.truth: B40 women-led households bear fiscal consolidation pain via squeezed social services and education, while male-dominated political/GLC networks continue rewarding patronage-based hiring.

class_truths_avoided.truth: Class cuts across race: connected elites of all ethnicities extract rents via GLCs/contracts while low-income Malays, Chinese, Indians, and Sabah/Sarawak natives bear the 1MDB debt through opportunity costs.

predictions.if_nothing_changes: By end-2027: 1MDB debt servicing still crowds out education/social spending; political financing bill still unpassed; new patronage scandals in run-up to state/general elections.
predictions.who_can_fix: Civil society (C4, IDEAS) + cross-party parliamentary pressure forcing transparency legislation.
predictions.why_they_wont: Ruling coalition benefits electorally from patronage machinery; voters reward immediate ethnic/contract benefits over long-term reform.
predictions.label: HYPOTHESIS

strongest_card.why: Audience reality check forces recognition that polished systemic analysis may feel distant to B40 workers.

weakest_card.why: Predictions generic; fails to specify exact incentives blocking current unity government.

overall_assessment: Refreshingly system-focused rather than Najib-centric; correctly nails cross-party patronage. But pulls punches on the current PH-BN unity government's active role in delaying reforms it once promised. Informs without fully confronting how ethnic clientelism and coalition math keep patronage machine humming.
